_description: ESPN team/player stats for Canada (3 group games + R32) and Morocco (3 group games + R32), R16 CAN vs MAR 2026-07-04. Using all 4 games since both teams have now played an R32 match -- most recent form weighted alongside group stage.

matches.note: Qatar had 2 red cards -- extreme blowout outlier, not representative

notes: SOT floor is remarkably consistent and high: 4, 10, 7, 7 across all 4 games -- NEVER below 4 SOT in any match, including their loss to Switzerland and their tense R32 win over South Africa. This looks like a durable shot-creation trait, not opponent-dependent variance.
Jonathan David: goals 0, 3, 0, 0 -- all 3 group goals came in the Qatar blowout (Qatar had 2 red cards, extreme outlier). Zero goals in 3 'normal' competitive games (BIH draw, SUI loss, RSA R32 win).

Alphonso Davies: appearances 0, 0, 0, 1 -- did NOT PLAY AT ALL in the first 3 group games (not benched-with-cameo, literally zero minutes recorded). First appearance of the tournament was a substitute cameo vs South Africa, recording 0 shots. Likely returning from injury/rotation -- his involvement level and output are both extremely uncertain for this match.
Very clean disciplinary record: 0.5 YC/game across 4 games -- consistently low-card team.
Achraf Hakimi: goals 0,0,1,0; assists 0,0,1,0 -- both involvements came in the same Haiti blowout game. Zero production in the 3 more competitive games (BRA, SCO, NED R32).

Ismael Saibari: goals 1,1,1,0 -- scored in 3 straight group games (vs BRA, SCO, HAI) but blanked (0 shots) in the R32 game vs Netherlands, his highest-quality opponent to date. Direct precedent: NED-MAR Q11 (Saibari score-or-assist) priced 0.32 vs crowd 0.35, outcome NO, +8.0 RBP -- confirms he's Morocco's primary channel but not automatic.

Brahim Díaz: SOT 1,0,0,0 across 4 games -- only 1 shot on target in the ENTIRE tournament so far despite starting every game. A much weaker underlying signal than his general 'creative Morocco starter' profile would suggest.
